2003-11-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: No Apology For Anti-Jewish Remarks
2003-11-01.abstract: Martin Hohmann, Christian Democrat legislator, refuses to apologize for speech he gave in Neuhof (Germany) in which he compared behavior and killing by Jews during Russian Revolution to Nazi murder of Europe's Jews during holocaust; German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's Social Democrats press for Hohmann's dismissal from Parliament (S)

2003-11-01.title: European poll calls Israel a threat to world peace
2003-11-01.abstract: PARIS:—  Almost 60 percent of Europeans say that Israel is a larger threat to world peace than North Korea, Iran or Afghanistan, according to a poll scheduled to be made public Monday by the European Commission.

2003-11-01.title: Malaysia Today
2003-11-01.abstract: Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamed leaves Malaysia with a strong economy. The following World Bank data highlight the state of his nation:

2003-11-01.title: RUSSIAN PREMIER STEPS INTO FRAY ON JAILED TYCOON
2003-11-01.abstract: Russian Prime Min Mikhail M Kasyanov, defying Pres Vladimir V Putin's warning against ministerial meddling in case of jailed Yukos Oil Co chairman Mikhail B Khodorkovsky, expresses 'deep concern' over freezing by prosecutors of large block of Yukos shares; stand by prominent pro-business official exposes deepening rift between government's free-market faction and officials drawn from Russia's security forces who favor stronger role for state over business; other businessmen who made fortunes out of chaotic 1990's privatizations seem increasingly fearful that government wants to curb big business (M)

2003-11-01.title: Congressional Unit Analyzes Military Costs in Iraq

2003-11-02.title: Ex-Guatemalan Rebel Tries Her Hand at Democracy
2003-11-02.abstract: Makrina Gudiel, former guerrilla in Guatemala who spent five years in exile in US, is congressional candidate of Guatemalan National Revolutionary Unity party in Nov 9 elections; rebels founded party after peace accords in 1996 ended country's 36-year civil war; profile; photo; map (M)

2003-11-02.title: A Former Iraqi Officer, Denied His Old Post, Fumes at the U.S.
2003-11-02.abstract: Iraqis are split on notion of recalling members of Iraqi Army that was disbanded by decree six months ago; Lt Col Amer Abdullah al-Rubaie, with degree in military science and 19 years as officer, scoffs at idea of throwing his career away or signing up as noncommissioned officer, but some Governing Council members say it would be mistake to activate old Iraqi Army; Rubaie argues that it would be easier for Iraqis to stabilize and secure country than it is for Americans; photo (M)

2003-11-02.title: U.S. Copter Is Downed in Iraq, Killing 16 and Wounding 20
2003-11-02.abstract: The deadliest single incident for Americans since the U.S. invaded Iraq was caused by a missile.

2003-11-02.title: New Malaysian Leader's Style Stirs Optimism
2003-11-02.abstract: Profile of Abdullah Badawi, new prime minister of Malaysia; Badawi succeeds Mahathir Mohammad and will likely keep basic policies that provide continuum for prosperous nation that survived Asian economic crisis better than most and is headed for nearly 5 percent growth this year; photo (M)

2003-11-02.title: In the Ranks, Similarities Between Vietnam and Iraq
2003-11-02.abstract: Article compares soldiers fighting in Iraq with those who fought in Vietnam by race, age, marital status, gender and other factors; map; graph (M)

2003-11-03.title: Baden-Baden Journal; Spoiled Russians. A New Belle Époque?
2003-11-03.abstract: Wealthy Russians are returning to Baden-Baden, German hot-springs resort city that became famous around world in 19th century when it was visited and written about by such notable Russian writers as Dostoyevsky, Tolstoy and Turgenev; Russians' return to scene they once helped make famous is sign of Russia's post-Soviet return to world; photo; map (M)

2003-11-03.title: African Anglicans Vent Anger at Gay Bishop
2003-11-03.abstract: Africas Anglican leaders expressed fury today at the consecration over the weekend of a gay bishop in New Hampshire.

2003-11-03.title: Russia Is Mostly Unmoved by the Troubles of Its Tycoons
2003-11-03.abstract: Residents of Khimki, Russia, show little sympathy for jailed oil tycoon Mikhail B Khodorkovsky and care little about freezing of shares in his company; distrust of wealthy runs deep in Russian society, with its roots in peasant culture; sociologist says Khodorkovsky's arrest might have appealed to other, dark prejudices as well; notes his father is Jewish, as are most of Russia's other tycoons; Russian government has long since given up state-sponsored anti-Semitism, but it might have made it more palatable to public that still harbors some deep-seated anti-Jewish sentiments; photo (M)

2003-11-03.title: Attack on U.S. Copter Highlights a Constant Threat Faced by Aircraft in Iraq
2003-11-03.abstract: Scope of threat facing American aircraft in Iraq is staggering; defensive tactics and devices have helped United States aircraft largely escape disaster until missile attack on helicopter that killed 16 American soldiers; American military commanders say nearly one million tons of weapons and ammunition remain at thousands of sites around Iraq, and only 100 of most sensitive sites are secured around the clock; say looters have ransacked many others (M)

2003-11-03.title: Religious Leader In Iran Praises Nuclear Pact
2003-11-03.abstract: Iran's supreme religious leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei lauds agreement on country's nuclear program that was reached with foreign ministers of Germany, Britain and France on Oct 21; country's hard-liners have harshly criticized decision, but Khamenei says Iran needs to prove to world that it is not seeking nuclear weapons (S)

2003-11-04.title: Iraq's Economy Is Mostly Stagnant, Propped Up by U.S. Cash and by Hopes of Safer Days
2003-11-04.abstract: Economy in Iraq is largely in stasis, propped up by American cash but showing little progress; privatization of decrepit state-owned industries is on hold; foreign investors have stayed away; still, Iraqis seem surprisingly, perhaps irrationally, hopeful about their economic prospects; survey of small and mid-size companies finds 57 percent of them expecting their prospects to improve over next six months; photo (M)

2003-11-04.title: Senate Sends Spending Bill For War Costs To President
2003-11-04.abstract: Senate gives its final approval to Pres Bush's request for $87.5 billion to occupy and rebuild Iraq and Afghanistan; this completes Congressional action on largest emergency spending bill ever sought by a president; misgivings raised by members of both parties to size of price tag are swept away by administration's ability to frame vote as test of support for American troops, and for global campaign against terrorism (M)

2003-11-04.title: Europe Warns of Trade Retaliation Against U.S.
2003-11-04.abstract: The European Union trade commissioner said today that trade retaliation by was "a racing certainty in mid-December."

2003-11-04.title: Diplomatic Tiff Over Ship's Virus
2003-11-04.abstract: British liner Aurora has been steaming Mediterranean from crisis to crisis after discovery that more than 550 of 1,800 passengers have highly contagious virus; Greek authorities take unusual step of putting ship in quarantine, and Spain, citing public health, closes its border (S)

2003-11-04.title: Arrests of Dissident Iranians Seen as Hard-Line Retaliation
2003-11-04.abstract: Two sons of prominent Iranian dissident cleric Grand Ayatollah Hossein Ali Montazeri are arrested after defying ban on use of office in religious city of Qum; arrest seems part of new crackdown on reformist activists as tension between reformers and hard-liners increases ahead of parliamentary elections scheduled on Feb 20 (M)

2003-11-05.title: As Tycoons Slip Overseas, Putin Appears To Lay Siege
2003-11-05.abstract: Lawyer for Russian oil tycoon Mikhail B Khodorkovsky says Moscow court will decide whether to release him on bail; pressure on his company continues with some Yukos executives leaving country and Pres Vladimir Putin bluntly charging tax evasion by unidentified officer; Americans now at helm of Yukos are Russian-American chief executive Simon G Kukes, chief operating office Steven M Theede and chief financial officer Bruce K Misamore; photo; about five of Russia's dozen tycoons are out of country amid nervousness about Putin's intentions (S)

2003-11-05.title: Officials Say U.S. Has No Plans for Iraqi Paramilitary Forces
2003-11-05.abstract: Occupying forces have no plans to use privately controlled Iraqi paramilitary forces to augment Iraqs army and police force.

2003-11-05.title: News Analysis: In Germany, the scent of regret
2003-11-05.abstract: BERLIN:—  Considering that the source was the chief of the European Union section of a government-subsidized policy research institute, the admonition was fairly extraordinary: Germany must not fall further into a French trap that was turning the Schröder government away from the country's real interests and potential for leadership in Europe.

2003-11-05.title: German General Fired for Backing Slur on Jews
2003-11-05.abstract: German Gen Reinhard Gunzel, commander of special forces unit, is dismissed after he praises conservative member of parliament, Martin Hohmann, for anti-Semitic remarks; Defense Min Peter Struck explains ouster; Hohmann called Jews 'race of perpetrators' (M)

2003-11-05.title: World Briefing | Americas: Canada: Canadian Sent To Syria By U.S. Wants Inquiry
2003-11-05.abstract: Canadian citizen Maher Arar, who was deported to his native Syria in 2002 by US as terrorism suspect, calls for inquiry into role Canada played in his ordeal; passing through Kennedy Airport when detained, he requested he be sent to Canada; US officials contend they acted in accordance with Canadian law enforcement information when they sent Arar to Syria; Arar describes his one-year imprisonment and torture in Syria as unimaginable; photo (S)

2003-11-06.title: U.N. Panel Puts Off Vote on Measures to Restrict Cloning
2003-11-06.abstract: Delegates have been unable to reach consensus on two pending resolutions that propose bans on human cloning, one of the most divisive nonpolitical issues among member states.

2003-11-06.title: Critics Mobilize and London Police Brace for Bush's Visit
2003-11-06.abstract: President Bush, who has been shielded from protests in recent travels, arrives in Britain on a state visit in two weeks, and the police here are weighing how to control promised street demonstrations without resorting to crowd control measures that could be seen as curbing free expression.    ''There will be substantial demonstrations over President Bush's visit -- as much as 50,000 to 60,000 people,'' Sir John Stevens, the Scotland Yard chief, told the Police Complaints Authority. ''Apart from ensuring his safety, which is our primary concern, we have to ensure the demonstrations are allowed to take place in the normal way we do in this democracy.''

2003-11-06.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Firefighter Strikes
2003-11-06.abstract: Unofficial firefighter strikes across England, to protest changes in new contract, coincide with Guy Fawkes Day; holiday, known for setting off fireworks and lighting bonfires, generally keeps firefighters busy (S)

2003-11-06.title: Iraq Said to Have Tried to Reach Last-Minute Deal to Avert War
2003-11-06.abstract: As American soldiers massed on the Iraqi border in March and diplomats argued about war, an influential adviser to the Pentagon received a secret message from a Lebanese-American businessman: Saddam Hussein wanted to make a deal.    Iraqi officials, including the chief of the Iraqi Intelligence Service, had told the businessman that they wanted Washington to know that Iraq no longer had weapons of mass destruction, and they offered to allow American troops and experts to conduct a search. The businessman said in an interview that the Iraqis also offered to hand over a man accused of being involved in the World Trade Center bombing in 1993 who was being held in Baghdad. At one point, he said, the Iraqis pledged to hold elections.

2003-11-06.title: World Briefing: Americas
2003-11-06.abstract: CANADA: Premier Rejects Deportation Inquiry; MEXICO: Mayor Defies Order To Pay Claim;.

2003-11-07.title: Soros Raided in Moscow in Property Dispute
2003-11-07.abstract: Commandos broke into offices of Soros in what a foundation official said was part of a legal dispute over the building.

2003-11-07.title: Sending the Guard
2003-11-07.abstract: With the Army stretched thin by duties in Iraq and Afghanistan, some of the Old Guards troops were recently assigned to a new and unexpected mission.

2003-11-07.title: Putin Says Oilman's Arrest Is No Threat to Private Enterprise
2003-11-07.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin, in heated news conference, emphasizes that arrest of Mikhail B Khodorkovsky, who owns giant Yukos Oil company, does not mean Russia would reverse its policies of private ownership; analysts and investors have been debating whether arrest signals broad crackdown or even rollback in privatization of state property in 1990's; Putin calls Khodorkovsky case 'isolated criminal matter'; notes high-ranking officials in Interior Ministry were arrested too; says aim is to instill law and order and fight corruption; Russian expert Prof Michael McFaul says targets of campaign are hustling entrepreneurs who seized much of Russia's natural resources during last decade; Khodorkovsky has angered Kremlin with his public activities, financing opposition parties and charitable foundations as well as academic institutions (M)

2003-11-07.title: Anwar Ibrahim's lawyers press his case : With Mahathir retired, ex-deputy may go free
2003-11-07.abstract: SINGAPORE:—  Though his political nemesis has stepped aside to await history's judgment, Malaysia's former deputy prime minister, Anwar Ibrahim, remains in a tiny prison cell, awaiting his own shot at vindication.

2003-11-07.title: Outspoken Minister Resigns in Clash With Colombia's President
2003-11-07.abstract: Fernando Londono resigns as interior and justice minister of Colombia after making remarks disavowed by Pres Alvaro Uribe; Londono, with reputation for being outspoken and contentious, prompted nationwide consternation when he told Conservative Party congressmen that Uribe might quit and call early elections if they did not support his fiscal reforms (M)

2003-11-08.title: Mideast View: Bush Spoke More to U.S. Than to Us
2003-11-08.abstract: Mideast commentators largely dismiss Pres Bush's speech calling for more democracy in region, viewing it as more for US domestic consumption (S)

2003-11-08.title: 6 AMERICANS DIE IN COPTER CRASH IN HUSSEIN'S CITY
2003-11-08.abstract: Six American soldiers are killed in Tikrit when Black Hawk helicopter crashes and burns in reeds along Tigris River, after reportedly exploding in midair; photos; Pentagon suspects Iraqi grenade attack; third such loss in two weeks poses problem for commanders who rely on relatively ungainly helicopters to transport troops and materiel across Iraq; 16 deaths in Chinook crash in Falluja is single deadliest attack on US forces since invasion; Black Hawk was ferrying troops from Mosul; second Black Hawk lands unscathed (M)

2003-11-08.title: With a U.S. Nod, Turkey Says It Won't Send Force to Iraq
2003-11-08.abstract: Turkey, with quiet US agreement, withdraws offer to deploy troops to Iraq, ending Bush administration's almost entirely futile effort to bring in large foreign contingents; Iraq was once part of Ottoman Empire and suffered under Turkish rule; Kurds in north were particularly opposed (S)

2003-11-08.title: World Briefing: Africa
2003-11-08.abstract: CONGO REPUBLIC: Ebola Outbreak Suspected; ETHIOPIA: Rains Reduce Food Needs;.

2003-11-08.title: Mauritania's Election Pits Staunch U.S. Ally Against 5 Opponents
2003-11-08.abstract: Mauritania presidential election pits staunch American ally, Maaouya Ould Sid Ahmed Taya, against five opponents, including Muhammad Khouna Ould Haidalla, former president drawing support from Arab nationalists, Islamists and Baath Party loyalists; Taya government foiled recent Islamist coup; impoverished Islamic republic straddles Arab and black Africa (M)

2003-11-09.title: North Korea's Bomb: Untested but Ready, C.I.A. Concludes
2003-11-09.abstract: CIA tells Congress that it now believes that North Korea has mastered technology of turning its nuclear fuel into functioning weapons, without having to prove their effectiveness through nuclear tests (M)

2003-11-09.title: Al Qaeda Blamed in Deadly Attack on Saudi Homes
2003-11-09.abstract: The casualty toll leaped to 17 dead and 122 wounded in the attack in the Saudi capital that officials attributed to Al Qaeda.

2003-11-09.title: Release of Berkeley Professor Is Delayed by Irans Judiciary
2003-11-09.abstract: The hard-line judiciary has refused to release Dariush Zahedi even though the Foreign Ministry said a week ago that he was about to be freed.

2003-11-09.title: A Former Rulers Candidacy Revives Fears in Guatemala
2003-11-09.abstract: Sunday is Election Day, and a once reviled and now revived old dictator stands a chance of regaining power.

2003-11-09.title: Lucky Break for Soldiers Heading Home From Iraq Turns to Heartbreak for Families
2003-11-09.abstract: Grief of families of 16 American soldiers killed in missile attack on their helicopter on Nov 2 is all the greater for some families because soldiers were on their way home after six months in Iraq; others are comforted by idea that soldiers' final hours were given over to cheerful anticipation of homecoming, not survival in combat; photo (M)

2003-11-10.title: U.S. Moves to Block Money For Troops Held in '91 War
2003-11-10.abstract: Bush administration wants to block group of American troops who were tortured in Iraqi prisons during Persian Gulf war in 1991 from collecting any of hundreds of millions of dollars in frozen Iraqi assets they won last summer in federal court ruling against government of Saddam Hussein; administration lawyers argue that frozen Iraqi assets in US banks are needed for Iraqi reconstruction and that judgment won by 17 former American prisoners should be overturned; administration is winning its court challenge so far but is not eager to publicize that fact; photo (M)

2003-11-10.title: Poppies, Rocks, Shards of Trouble
2003-11-10.abstract: For the past two weeks German soldiers have been securing northeastern Afghanistan, one of the biggest opium-growing areas in the world. But there they are safe only so long as they dont get in the way of the local chieftains and their booming drug trade. Orders to the troops are: Look the other way.

2003-11-10.title: Fresh Blood in the Theocracy
2003-11-10.abstract: In the nuclear poker game with the West, a third faction is exerting its influence in the power struggle between the right and the reformers: the pragmatic conservatives.

2003-11-10.title: U.S. Forces in Airborne Assault on Rebels in 2 Afghan Areas
2003-11-10.abstract: American forces opened an assault against suspected Taliban forces in two northeastern provinces over the weekend.

2003-11-10.title: U.S. Aides Acknowledge String of Missteps With Turkey
2003-11-10.abstract: Bush administration officials are shrugging off what some acknowledge to be series of mistakes in relations with Turkey since before war in Iraq; last Feb, Turkey rejected American appeals to allow American soldiers to go through Turkish territory to fight in northern Iraq, just few months after Deputy Defense Sec Paul D Wolfowitz claimed Turkish assent was 'assured'; supporters of Wolfowitz and supporters of Secretary of State Colin Powell blamed each other; now those recriminations are playing out again as Turkey decides not to send troops to help patrol Iraq (M)

2003-11-11.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Inquiry Into Baby Formula Deaths
2003-11-11.abstract: Israeli Health Ministry announces three babies have died and at least 17 more are seriously ill after receiving non-dairy baby formula made by German firm Humana; product did not contain needed vitamin B1, which was listed on label; product has been pulled from shelves and police have opened criminal investigation; company chief Rolf Janshen maintains product tested at plant contains B1 (S)

2003-11-11.title: Bush Links Efforts in Iraq and Afghanistan to U.S. Security
2003-11-11.abstract: Marking Veterans Day, President Bush said the people serving in Afghanistan and Iraq today are helping "democracy, peace and justice rise in a violent and troubled region."

2003-11-11.title: Barrier Route Would Give Israel 15% of West Bank, U.N. Says
2003-11-11.abstract: Israeli officials questioned the report and said the government was still assessing how many Palestinians would be affected.

2003-11-11.title: Russia Ready to Aid Iran With A-Plant
2003-11-11.abstract: Hassan Rowhani, leader of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, visiting Russia, says Iran has halted its uranium-enrichment program and will sign protocol that will be safeguard against its development of nuclear arms; says Iran will agree to more intrusive International Atomic Energy Agency inspections on short notice; Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin, after meeting with Rowhani, says he sees no obstacles to cooperation on nuclear power plant Russians are building in Bushehr (S)

2003-11-11.title: Iraqi Security Forces Outnumber American Troops, U.S. Says
2003-11-11.abstract: A top U.S. military official said today that Iraqis taking part in security jobs now outnumber American troops.

2003-11-12.title: Survivors of Riyadh Bombing Pick Up Pieces of Their Lives
2003-11-12.abstract: Dany Ibrahim and Houry Haytayan, Lebanese couple living in Saudi Arabia, recall neighbor, also expecting first child, who was killed in terrorist bombing that destroyed their homes in Riyadh; photo; Saudi official reports arrest of some suspects; London-based Arabic newspaper publishes message from Al Qaeda operative claiming responsibility; threat of new attack keeps US Embassy closed (M)

2003-11-12.title: Afghans' Political Prognosis Is Still Hazy, U.N. Panel Says
2003-11-12.abstract: German Amb Gunter Pleuger, who led UN Security Council mission to Afghanistan, reports that terrorism, drug-related crime and factional fighting threaten to reverse advances in rebuilding and efforts to form democratic government; comments follow car bombing outside UN office in Kandahar; Pleuger says Taliban, Al Qaeda and warlord Gulbuddin Hekmatyar pose 'significant threat' to national security forces (M)

2003-11-12.title: Commander of Colombias Armed Forces Steps Down
2003-11-12.abstract: The commander of Colombias armed forces is resigning, joining three cabinet ministers who have stepped down in a shake-up marring President Álvaro Uribes administration.

2003-11-12.title: Senate Follows House and Votes to Impose Sanctions Against Syria
2003-11-12.abstract: Senate, 89-4, joins House in endorsing diplomatic and economic sanctions against Syria, unless government halts flow of fighters and weapons into Iraq (S)

2003-11-12.title: World Briefing | Africa: Kenya: Mau Mau Group Recognized
2003-11-12.abstract: Kenya overturns 1950's colonial-era legislation labeling members of Mau Mau movement as terrorists; Vice President Moody Awori praises group as brave in their defense of freedom (S)

2003-11-13.title: Iraqi Leaders Seek Power Before Drafting a Charter
2003-11-13.abstract: Iraqi political leaders have decided a plan to write a new constitution would be too divisive now, and propose instead that they immediately assume the powers of a provisional government.

2003-11-13.title: U.S. Moves to Speed Up Iraqi Vote and Shift of Power
2003-11-13.abstract: Bush administration moves up its timetable for self-government in Iraq, concluding that increasing attacks on American and other foreign forces lend urgency to need for Iraqi self-rule by mid-2004; administration will try to hold elections in first half of next year and turn civilian authority over to temporary government before new constitution is written; new plan is intended to change political climate in Iraq and reduce anger toward occupying forces that fosters support for violence, by demonstrating to Iraqis that US is moving more quickly to establish self-rule; it is not clear whether those behind guerrilla attacks would regard changed political situation as significant if large numbers of American forces remain in Iraq; American administrator L Paul Bremer 3d, in Washington for high-level talks, heads back to Iraq to begin consultations about new American plan with Iraqi leaders, who have grown increasingly impatient with American-led occupation; members of Iraqi Governing Council say they will reject any plan to write new constitution in coming months because that would be too divisive; say they will propose instead that they immediately assume powers of provisional government (M)

2003-11-13.title: Iraqi Leaders Seek Power Before Drafting a Charter
2003-11-13.abstract: Iraqi political leaders reject plan to write new constitution in coming months, saying it would be too divisive now; they will propose instead that they immediately assume powers of provisional government; say they will work on drafting 'basic law' in hope that they can win international recognition for Iraqi government that would take over considerable authority from American administrator L Paul Bremer 3d and meet conditions laid down by United Nations Security Council last month; their push to establish provisional government reflects frustration with American-led organization overseeing Iraq (M)

2003-11-13.title: At Least 26 Killed in a Bombing Of an Italian Compound in Iraq
2003-11-13.abstract: Car or truck bomb explodes in courtyard of Italian paramilitary police headquarters in Nasiriya, Iraq, killing 17 Italians and at least 9 Iraqis and wounding more than 105 others; it is most lethal single attack in series of attacks on Americans and other foreigners and Iraqis who support them since Saddam Hussein was swept from power in Apr; buildings were only lightly protected; hours after latest blast, American forces launch pair of ferocious strikes against suspected loyalists of Saddam Hussein's government in Baghdad, signaling new and more aggressive strategy; in Rome, Italian Defense Min Antonio Martino blames loyalists of Hussein for attack but presents no evidence to support his claim; Italian Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi says Italy would not be shaken from its commitment to Iraq and United States; photos of attack in Nasiriya, and of previous major bombings against foreign targets in Iraq (M)

2003-11-13.title: Iran's Leader Says U.N. Report Removes Suspicions of Weapons
2003-11-13.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami says recent report on Iran's nuclear programs by International Atomic Energy Agency clears Iran of suspicions that it is developing nuclear weapons; adds that Tehran might end its voluntary cooperation if agency's next report, due Nov 20, bends to political pressures; IAEA report states that Iran's nuclear program goes back 18 years, much earlier than what Tehran claims; it also says Iran has produced small amounts of material, including plutonium, that could be made into weapons; report concludes that because of Iran's past pattern of concealment it would take some time before it could determine if its nuclear program is intended exclusively for peaceful purposes; in Washington, deputy undersecretary of state John R Bolton calls agency's finding that there is no immediate evidence Iran has nuclear weapons program 'impossible to believe' (M)

2003-11-14.title: 4 Israeli Intelligence Experts Call for Political Solution
2003-11-14.abstract: It was the latest in a series of statements by officials and soldiers questioning Israels policy toward the Palestinians.

2003-11-14.title: In U.S., Fears Are Voiced Of a Too-Rapid Iraq Exit
2003-11-14.abstract: Bush administration decision to speed transfer of sovereignty to Iraqis and replace American troops with Iraqis draws warnings from Congress and policy experts against pulling out too early and allowing election-year politics dictate policy; Republican Sens Lindsey Graham and John McCain are among those expressing doubts (M)

2003-11-14.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-11-14.abstract: GERMANY: Qaeda Suspect May Be Handed Over To U.S.; RUSSIA: Army Diet Found Lacking; SERBIA: Elections Called;.

2003-11-14.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-11-14.abstract: List of Americans newly confirmed dead in Iraqï

2003-11-14.title: Air Raid Sends Iraqis Message, But What Is It?
2003-11-14.abstract: American commandersin Iraq say their well-publicized offensive against Iraqi insurgents is meant to send rebels 'a message'; at site of one strike in Baghdad, local Iraqis wonder what message is; owner of factory that was bombed says Americans warned guards to leave building before attack; American commanders say they were certain building had been used to fire mortars at American soldiers; commanders concede their primary aim was to impress guerrillas as much as to kill them; photo (M)

2003-11-15.title: A political brawl rages at Académie Française
2003-11-15.abstract: PARIS:—  A bitter political fight has broken out at the pinnacle of the French literary world over whether Valéry Giscard d'Estaing, the former French president, is an acceptable choice for the Académie Française.

2003-11-15.title: President of Georgia Pleads For Calm as Protests Grow
2003-11-15.abstract: Georgian Pres Eduard A Shevardnadze calls for calm as thousands of protesters fill streets, surrounding presidential compound in Tiblisi and calling for his resignation; warns protests could spark civil war and rebuffs demands that he resign; protests began over manipulated parliamentary election on Nov 2 and have swelled into cry of anger over decade of mismanagement, during which country has slid into poverty and corruption; opposition leader Mikhail Saakashvili calls for campaign of civil disobedience, urging demonstrators to remain peaceful; map; photos (M)

2003-11-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: First Nuclear Plant Shut
2003-11-15.abstract: German Environmental Ministry announces closure of first of nation's 19 nuclear power plants; all of plants are scheduled to be phased out over next two decades; 32-year-old plant in Stade is off-line and dismantling process is due to begin in 2005; France will reprocess spent nuclear fuel rods from facility (S)

2003-11-15.title: Joy, and Jeers, As New Police Patrol Baghdad
2003-11-15.abstract: Brig Gen Ahmed Ibrahim, Iraq's deputy interior minister and boss of national police, along with Baghdad police chief Hassan al-Obeidi, asks residents of Baghdad to help fight crime; say merchants should let them know if they see anything suspicious; very visible raids and patrols are being conducted to help foster sense of safety; photos (M)

2003-11-15.title: India's Poor Bet Precious Sums on Private Schools
2003-11-15.abstract: Private schools are springing up in India, capitalizing on parental dismay over poor quality of government schools; quality is uneven, but schools are considered best choice and instruct in English; poor parents seek out private schools, certain that if they provide right education, children will rise above their circumstances; some say such expenditures by poor represent disgraceful abdication by state, one that creates class system segregating those with private education from those without and say such education should be free; some predict that within 10 to 15 years, government schooling will be eliminated; Indian law calls for free compulsory education for children up to 14, but does not address quality of government schools, which may lack power, latrines and even teachers, and do not teach in English, language considered vital for good future employment; photos (L)

2003-11-16.title: German City Tries to Persuade Pentagon to Keep Bases Open
2003-11-16.abstract: Officials in Mainz, Germany, are scrambling to avert what they fear could be economic blow to region as United States rethinks deployment of nearly 63,000 soldiers and their families in Rhineland-Palatinate, state of which Mainz is capital; bases put estimated $1.4 billion annually into economy and generate 27,000 jobs; with talk of base closings, delegation from Mainz travels to Pentagon to make pitch for troops to stay; such appeals are unlikely to carry much weight in light of Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld's call for more agility and better strategic planning but move is not thought to be imminent, as resources are focused on Iraq; map (M)

2003-11-16.title: A Muslim Scholar Raises Hackles in France
2003-11-16.abstract: Profile of Tariq Ramadan, Swiss-born philosophy professor who touched off controversy last month when he criticized French Jewish intellectuals for supporting American-led war in Iraq; to Muslim followers in France, where he is attending conference, he is spiritual guide; to experts on Islam, he is respectable scholar who promotes moderate version of Islam from his headquarters in Geneva and in his lectures around world; but to some French intellectuals, he is dangerous demagogue whose words have multiple meanings and are laced with anger; Ramadan says he is misunderstood and denies being anti-Semitic, attributing campaign against him to his popularity; photo (M)

2003-11-16.title: Serbia Will Try, for a Third Time, to Elect a President
2003-11-16.abstract: Serbia will try to elect president for third time in about 14 months; post has been vacant for year but two previous elections were declared void because required minimum of electorate did not vote; six candidates are running, with Dragoljub Micunovic favored to win; coalition government has effectively collapsed and Parliament was recently dissolved, with new elections scheduled for Dec 28; photo (M)

2003-11-16.title: Rumsfeld Presses Japan on U.S. Suspects' Rights
2003-11-16.abstract: Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld says it is urgent to resolve festering issue of defining legal rights for American military personnel in Japan suspected of violent crimes, especially rape and murder; talks on issue were disbanded in August; US wants guarantees that its troops will be protected by legal rights similar to those they would receive at home, but Japan balks at giving Americans more legal privileges than its own citizens (M)

2003-11-16.title: Argentine Moves Against Police Corruption
2003-11-16.abstract: President Néstor Kirchner seems to be meeting stiff resistance as he tries to take on the countrys corrupt police force.

2003-11-17.title: Turkish Police Expect to Identify 2 Suicide Bombers
2003-11-17.abstract: One more body was pulled from the rubble left by the attacks on two synagogues, raising the death toll to 24 people.

2003-11-17.title: A real innocent
2003-11-17.abstract: During the Iraq war, Jessica Lynch became a symbol of a glorious military campaign. In her recently published book, she is portrayed as a country girl who naively stumbled into a foreign war. Now, once again, she is a symbol for Americas state of mind.

2003-11-17.title: Catalan Voters Lean Left
2003-11-17.abstract: Elections in Catalonia leave minor nationalist party, Republican Left, holding key to control of regional parliament (S)

2003-11-17.title: Palestinian Sides Agree to Cease-Fire Talks
2003-11-17.abstract: The announcement came as Ariel Sharon, said that he might meet the Palestinian prime minister "in the next few days."

2003-11-17.title: 2 Yemenis Tied to Al Qaeda Extradited by Germany to U.S.
2003-11-17.abstract: Two Yemenis accused of funneling money to Al Qaeda been extradited to the U.S. from Germany to face criminal charges.

2003-11-18.title: Bush Insists That U.S. Troops Will Stay in Iraq
2003-11-18.abstract: Pres Bush says emphatically that United States will not leave Iraq even though White House has decided to speed transfer of American civilian authority to new government in Baghdad; comments are his most explicit to date about keeping American troops in Iraq, but he does not say how long they will stay, or how many will remain (S)

2003-11-18.title: Mexicos Envoy to U.N. Is Fired After Criticizing U.S. Policy
2003-11-18.abstract: Mexicos ambassador to the U.N. has been dismissed after saying the U.S. regards Mexico as a second-class country.

2003-11-18.title: We Welcome You To Lush Zimbabwe! Your Wallet, Please!
2003-11-18.abstract: Police and paramilitary squads in Zimbabwe are said to be seizing foreign money from tourists and moneychangers in effort to allay acute shortage of hard currency in country; reports in government-controlled press say foreign currency is being collected to buy gasoline for farm vehicles, but unidentified Zimbabwean claims government is buying fuel for its military as precaution against civil unrest (S)

2003-11-18.title: Diversity at the table / A fight against blandness: The Brillat-Savarins of slow food: They're eating well and doing right
2003-11-18.abstract: NAPLES—  Slow Food, one of the world's largest and most avant-garde alliances of food lovers, gathered more than 600 protesters of fast food from 36 countries on a recent weekend in Naples to assail blandness and to extol "eco-gastronomy," authentic food traditions and third world peasants.

2003-11-18.title: Turkey Links Synagogue Bombers to Al Qaeda
2003-11-18.abstract: Turkey said today that the bombers who killed at least 25 people on Saturday were Turkish citizens with links to Al Qaeda.

2003-11-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Breakaway Church In Talks
2003-11-19.abstract: Russian Orthodox Church Outside of Russia is holding first-ever talks in Russia to explore reuniting New York based group with Moscow Patriarchate (S)

2003-11-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Turkey: Go-Ahead For Kurdish Broadcasts
2003-11-19.abstract: Turkish officials reveal guidelines for Kurdish-language media, as part of Parliament's expanded rights program designed, in part, to satisfy European Union's list of reforms necessary for membership in group (S)

2003-11-19.title: World Briefing | Africa: Annan Plea For Africa
2003-11-19.abstract: United Nations Secretary General Kofi Annan begins yearly fund raising appeal, citing need for $3 billion in aid for 2004 to help address 21 of world's most serious crises; 2003 appeal has received just 66% of needed funds (S)

2003-11-19.title: Palestinian Premier Starts Cease-Fire Bid
2003-11-19.abstract: Ahmed Qurei traveled to the Gaza Strip today in a bid to persuade Palestinian factions to halt attacks against Israel.

2003-11-19.title: Resurgent Taliban Threatens Afghan Stability, U.S. Says
2003-11-19.abstract: Bush administration officials say resurgence in Taliban activity and cross-border operation pose new threat to eastern and southern Afghanistan and new diplomatic challenge in American dealings with Pakistan; are hopeful that in time, American military operations will push Taliban back and that increase in economic assistance will pull people away from Taliban; say Pakistan has increased its military and security presence in Pakistani provinces bordering Afghanistan but much more is needed to prevent Taliban from using Pakistani territory as sanctuary and arms supply base (M)

2003-11-20.title: Of Blair and Bush, and the Ties That Bind
2003-11-20.abstract: Feeling among many Britons is that part of 'special relationship' between Britain and US based on shared language, history, culture and economy remains solid and enduring, but that political and diplomatic costs have become too high; Prime Min Tony Blair is gaining little at home from being pictured at Bush's side; Bush's presence serves to point up what divides two allies as well as what unites them; adding to British frustration is that there are no indications that Bush's visit will answer question about whether Blair's loyalty gains Britain any added consideration in Washington; photo (M)

2003-11-20.title: Barrier to Stay, Israel Declares
2003-11-20.abstract: Israel rejects criticism of barrier it is building in West Bank, in Pres Bush's speech in London; says says construction would proceed as planned (S)

2003-11-20.title: For Bushes, French Wine And U.S. Tunes
2003-11-20.abstract: Queen Elizabeth's state banquet for Pres Bush and wife Laura described (S)

2003-11-20.title: Chirac's anti-Semitism fight
2003-11-20.abstract: PARIS:—  With his condemnation of the rise of a new anti-Semitism in France, and his promises to combat it, President Jacques Chirac has conceded that his country has a troubled place among the European nations where anti-Jewish outbursts and attacks have become an undeniable and embarrassing pattern.

2003-11-20.title: More Proof of Iraq-Qaeda Link, or Not?
2003-11-20.abstract: Weekly Standard, journal with close ties to Bush administration hawks, reports on highly classified Oct 27 memo from Douglas J Feith, under secretary of defense, to Senate intelligence committee, listing 50 points of raw intelligence that, according to Feith, points to operational link between Iraq and Al Qaeda; conservatives are seizing on report as evidence that Iraq did indeed pose real danger to US; government officials with knowledge of intelligence on Iraq say reports cited by Feith are authentic, but they are not new and some are not credible (M)

2003-11-21.title: Turkey Arrests Suspects in 2 Bomb Attacks in Istanbul
2003-11-21.abstract: Foreign governments warned of more attacks and groups apparently linked to Al Qaeda claimed responsibility.

2003-11-21.title: Mexicos Ambassador to the U.N. Resigns
2003-11-21.abstract: The envoy, Adolfo Aguilar Zinser, said the Bush administration had prodded the administration of Mexican President Vicente Fox to remove him.

2003-11-21.title: Officials in Georgia Declare Shevardnadze Ballot Winner
2003-11-21.abstract: Election Commission of Georgia announces that supporters of Pres Eduard A Shevardnadze have won disputed parliamentary election, confirming result that has already sent thousands of protesters into streets; opposition leaders renew calls for unpopular president to step down(S)

2003-11-21.title: Slain Frenchwoman Buried in Afghanistan
2003-11-21.abstract: Bettina Goislard, 29-year-old French refugee worker killed by suspected Taliban gunman, is buried in British Cemetery in Kabul, in accordance with her wishes to be buried in Afghanistan (S)

2003-11-21.title: Kurilovo Journal; From Village Boy to Soldier, Martyr and, Many Say, Saint
2003-11-21.abstract: Kurilovo Journal on young Russian soldier, Yevgeny Rodionov, whose death in Chechnya has made him national hero and unofficial saint, focus of minor cult; Rodionov is said to have been killed by Muslim rebels seven years ago, at age 19, because he refused to renounce his religion or remove cross around his neck; photos of his mother and icons of her son; map (M)

2003-11-22.title: French Official Looks in His Mirror and Sees Future President
2003-11-22.abstract: Min Nicolas Sarkozy breaks France's cardinal rule against flaunting ambition and admits on television that he thinks about running for president; is by far nation's most popular minister, with approval rating far higher than Pres Jacques Chirac's; Prime Min Jean-Pierre Raffarin has steadily dropped in polls, fueling speculation that he may be replaced, perhaps by Sarkozy; Sarkozy, during broadcast, also says he favors moves to help raise Muslims out of poor suburban ghettos and give them place in French society; photo (M)

2003-11-22.title: Insurgents Use Rockets on Donkey Carts to Hit Sites in Iraqi Capital
2003-11-22.abstract: Iraqi insurgents use homemade multiple rocketlaunchers on donkey carts disguised as hay wagons to carry out what American officers call 'spectacular' strikes against heavily fortified targets, Baghdad; get close to heavily defended sites, including two hotels and Oil Ministry; critically wound American civilian in hotel attack; donkey cart is spotted and disarmed before it could detonate near Italian and Turkish embassies and Kurdish political party headquarters; another is disarmed near American military camp; attacks seem intended to have maximum psychological impact on city's increasingly fearful residents; photos (M)

2003-11-22.title: Expat adviser : Dual options for health insurance
World Briefing | Asia: India: Court Halts Riot Trial
2003-11-22.abstract: Indian Supreme Court suspends trial of 10 people accused of involvement in last year's Hindu-Muslim rioting that killed at least 1,000 Muslims; gives local Gujarat State court two weeks to justify why cases were not transferred out of region; trials have been criticized as unjust (S)

2003-11-22.title: Officials Fear New Attacks by Militants in Southeast Asia
2003-11-22.abstract: American officials in Southeast Asia are bracing for new terrorist attacks as they gather new data on Jemaah Islamiyah, Indonesia-based radical Islamic group; they and Asian officials say group remains intact and is growing stronger despite arrests of some top leaders, interviews; say money and arms have flowed to group through Philippines, center for training and money laundering; see attacks most likely in Indonesia and Philippines, with Malaysia and Thailand lesser targets; cite new data gathered from interrogation of Taufik Rifki, group official seized in Oct in Mindanao, Philippines; photo (M)

2003-11-23.title: Iraq Picks American as Ambassador to U.S.
2003-11-23.abstract: Iraqi political leaders say they picked Rend Rahim Francke, Iraqi-American activist, as country's diplomatic representative in Washington; Iraq Foundation, which she helped create, has spent decade devising visions of democratic rule for Iraq and lobbying for war crimes trial of Saddam Hussein; she says her new job will be informal ambassadorship; her appointment reflects accelerating political developments in Iraq in past week since Bush administration said it would turn over control of nation to provisional government by June; Francke, who has not lived in Iraq full time since 1970's, became US citizen in 1987 but considers herself Iraqi citizen as well; photo (M)

2003-11-23.title: Iraqi Town Relishes Freedom, But Resentment Runs Beneath
2003-11-23.abstract: People of small Iraqi village of Kifl, all Shiites repressed under Saddam Hussein's rule--relish their new freedom but complain that their expectations of aid and compensation, democracy and security, remain unmet; beneath outward peace runs undercurrent of need and grievance and simmering resentment that has not boiled over but easily could; village is still recovering from damage of major battle that took place eight months ago, as American forces advanced on Baghdad; 11 people from Kifl died, and more than dozen homes were destroyed; Kifl has recovered more quickly than many places in Iraq, in large part because it has not experienced continuing resistance that has brought new destruction elsewhere and hampered efforts to rebuild; there was no frenzied looting in Kifl, which has little of value; election was recently held to create administrative council with 20 seats; in Kifl, as in other parts of Iraq, freedom and democracy seem beside the point to people with little money in their pockets; photos; map (L)

2003-11-23.title: Bowing to Opposition, Leader of Georgia Steps Down
2003-11-23.abstract: President Eduard A. Shevardnadze resigned today in the face of huge public protests over the corruption and economic collapse that have marked his 12-year rule.

2003-11-23.title: Bombers Kill 14 in Iraq; Missile Hits Civilian Plane
2003-11-23.abstract: American soldiers have been unable to snuff out continuing chaos and violence in Iraq; in Baghdad, missile hits civilian airplane, forcing it to make emergency landing; also in Baghdad, top Iraqi politician Abdel Aziz al-Hakim is attacked by mortar shell at mosque but he is not hurt; in Khan Bani Saad, north of Baghdad, suicide attackers explode huge bombs at police station, killing at least 14 people; continuing attacks on civilian flights that operate in Iraq may delay opening airport to civilian traffic, one of major markers for stability in country; photos (M)

2003-11-23.title: A Battlefield In Belgium Lifts a Curtain On the Past
2003-11-23.abstract: Archaeologists unveil once huge network of narrow dark trenches in potato field in Ypres, Belgium, providing grim testimony to way millions of terrified young soldiers struggled to hold on to life in World War I; trenches have yielded partial remains and wartime artifacts of seven soldiers, including brass regimental badge and uniform fragments almost certainly belonging to 21-year-old British lance corporal killed in Battle of Passendale in 1917; Belgian archaeologists and British historians are engaged in race against time; their excavation work must end next year to make way for highway project; photos; map (M)

2003-11-24.title: New Attacks in Iraq
2003-11-24.abstract: Three American soldiers were killed in Iraq, military officials said, including two who came under attack in the northern city of Mosul. In Baquba, north of Baghdad, where at least five people were killed in a suicide bombing on Saturday, a soldier from the Fourth Infantry Division was killed by a roadside bomb.    Article, Page A10.

2003-11-24.title: The Other Conflict Continues to Take a G.I. Toll
2003-11-24.abstract: Fatal clashes with shadowy enemy continue for American soldiers stationed in Afghanistan; guerrilla attacks have killed five American and four Afghan soldiers along border with Pakistan in last eight weeks; in series of strikes, pro-Taliban fighters launch missiles, plant mines and mount fierce ambushes against American forces within miles of Pakistan border, and then are seen retreating toward Pakistan; American officer notes difficulty for Pakistan of sealing off such mountainous terrain; battlefield is swath of dozens of miles of arid plateau in eastern Afghanistan, lined by hills and mountains to east and west; photos; map (M)

2003-11-24.title: A Troubled Past
2003-11-24.abstract: Georgia, the birthplace of Stalin, endured centuries of foreign domination. The Red Army forcibly made it a republic in 1921.

2003-11-24.title: GEORGIAN LEADER AGREES TO RESIGN, ENDING STANDOFF
2003-11-24.abstract: Pres Eduard A Shevardnadze of Georgia resigns in face of huge public protests over corruption and economic collapse that have marked his nearly 12-year rule; says he concluded that protests could not have ended bloodlessly; news of his departure prompts wild celebration in streets of capital, with fireworks exploding overhead and air filled with cheers; Mikhail Saakashvili, main opposition leader, says new parliamentary and presidential elections will be held and that there should be no retribution against Shevardnadze; parliamentary speaker Nino Burdzhanadze, another opposition leader, steps in as acting president, as mandated by Constitution, and says elections will be held within statutory period of 45 days; she and other opposition leaders speak with respect for Shevardnadze, who was major figure on world stage as Russian foreign minister under Mikhail S Gorbachev; his tenure as president of Georgia has been difficult, with separatist rebellions in four provinces and three assassination attempts; photo (M)

2003-11-24.title: 2 G.I.'s, Throats Slashed, Found Dead After Rock-Throwing Attack on Car in Northern City
2003-11-24.abstract: Three American soldiers are killed in Iraq; throats of two soldiers are slashed after they come under attack in northern city of Mosul; third soldier is killed in roadside bombing in Baquba, where at least five people are killed in suicide bombing; photo (M)

2003-11-25.title: Iraqis Ask U.N. For Resolution
2003-11-25.abstract: Iraqi Governing Council asks for UN Security Council resolution recognizing new American-Iraqi agreement for speedier transfer of power (S)

2003-11-25.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Rare Gorilla Dead
2003-11-25.abstract: Snowflake, 40-year-old rare albino gorilla who was born in Guinea, and has been living in captivity at Barcelona (Spain) zoo since 1966, is euthanized after losing battle with skin cancer; sired 21 off-spring of which none are albino; photo (S)

2003-11-25.title: World Briefing: Africa
2003-11-25.abstract: BURUNDI: Rebels Join Government; ZIMBABWE: Army Nurses Deployed In Hospital Strike;.

2003-11-25.title: U.S. Acquiesces to Allies on New Iran Nuclear Resolution
2003-11-25.abstract: US, bowing to wishes of allies, agrees to let International Atomic Energy Agency adopt resolution deploring Iran's nuclear program without referring issue to UN for possible sanctions; France, Britain, Germany and director Dr Mohamed ElBaradei argue that Iran's recent actions warrant more conciliatory approach (M)

2003-11-25.title: U.S. Presses Israel to Stop Work On New Settlements and Barrier
2003-11-25.abstract: Bush administration sends new signals demanding that Israel stop expansion of settlements and construction of barrier in West Bank; envoy Elliott Abrams conveyed concern to Prime Min Ariel Sharon at recent meeting in Rome; Egypt will convene meeting of Palestinian leaders to discuss reviving cease-fire (S)

2003-11-26.title: Interim Leaders in Georgia Back Presidential Candidate
2003-11-26.abstract: Mikhail Saakashvili, the leader of Georgias popular revolt, became the front-runner for the presidency today.

2003-11-26.title: Roh vetoes bill, enraging opposition in Seoul
2003-11-26.abstract: SEOUL:—  President Roh Moo Hyun, who has already rattled domestic politics through his maverick style of leadership, set the stage Tuesday for heightened tension with South Korea's powerful opposition party.

2003-11-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: President Vetoes Inquiry
2003-11-26.abstract: South Korean President Roh Moo Hyun vetoes bill calling for independent investigation into allegations of corruption by former presidential aides; opposition lawmakers stage sit-in to protest and Grand National Party leader says he will begin hunger strike (S)

2003-11-26.title: Arroyo rejects calls to lift moratorium on death penalty
2003-11-26.abstract: MANILA:—  President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo on Tuesday rejected calls for the reimposition of the death penalty, saying it had not been proved to deter crime in the Philippines.

2003-11-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: To the Polls
2003-11-26.abstract: Northern Irish voters cast ballots for province's Legislature for first time since 1998 peace accord, under which Protestant and Roman Catholic parties share political power; 108-seat Assembly has been suspended for past year (S)

2003-11-27.title: Bush Makes Surprise Thanksgiving Visit to Iraq
2003-11-27.abstract: President Bush flew into Baghdad today aboard Air Force One to have dinner with United States officials and a group of astonished American troops.

2003-11-27.title: Turkish Town's Despair Breeds Terrorists, Residents Fear
2003-11-27.abstract: At least two of suicide bombers who recently struck in Istanbul grew up in desolate town of Bingol in remote eastern Turkey; photos; map; devoutly Muslim, mostly Kurdish people there have little work, but share pervasive sense of oppression that gives rise to extremist ideologies offering some kind of purpose to young men; devastating earthquake last May has only deepened sense of grievance among residents who say government is slow and stinting with aid (M)

2003-11-27.title: U.S. to Shift Some Experts From Arms To Antiterror
2003-11-27.abstract: Dozens of US intelligence experts and linguists sent to Iraq to search for illicit weapons have been reassigned to urgent effort to counter insurgents attacking US troops; as many as 40 attacks are reported daily and US commanders know little about source (S)

2003-11-27.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Kashmir Guns Fall Silent
2003-11-27.abstract: India and Parkistan mark first 24 hours of Kashmir cease-fire without any reported violations (S)

2003-11-27.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-11-27.abstract: INDIA: 10,000 Flee Violence; INDIA: Kashmir Guns Fall Silent;.

2003-11-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Some Like Medals, Some Don't
2003-11-28.abstract: Britain's Queen Elizabeth II presents Order of British Empire to England's soccer team captain David Beckham; Rastafarian poet Benjamin Zephaniah, protesting what he sees as British imperialism, rejects same award (S)

2003-11-28.title: Report cites stigma and discrimination as bars to prevention: Fear fuels the spread of AIDS
2003-11-28.abstract: MANILA:—  The climate of fear surrounding AIDS is hampering efforts to curb its spread and creating the environment for the epidemic to worsen, the World Health Organization said.

2003-11-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Algerian Bomber's Conviction Upheld
2003-11-28.abstract: Algerian bomber Boualem Bensaid, who was convicted for his involvement in 1995 Paris bombing wave that killed 8 people and injured more than 200, does not win his appeal and will serve life sentence mandated by French court (S)

2003-11-28.title: Three Arrested as Part of European Terror Investigation
2003-11-28.abstract: Italian and German police arrested three north Africans in an investigation of a European-based network linked to al Qaeda.

2003-11-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Rise In Tobacco Prices May Ease
2003-11-28.abstract: French Senate approves revision of bill to increase price of tobacco after thousands protest change; new proposal increases prices 8 to 10 percent rather than 20 percent orignally approved by lower house (S)

2003-11-29.title: Building a crèche set, one figurine at a time
2003-11-29.abstract: VIENNA—  Two years ago, I was hurrying through Vienna's cheerful, kitschy Christkindl Markt looking for something worth buying when I noticed a little wooden stall right under the giant Christmas tree in front of the Rathaus. The storefront was full of tiny wooden figures, angels, saints and crèche figurines. When I asked the price, I was startled by how expensive they were — until I took another look at the detailed, expressive faces, the soft, glowing colors.

2003-11-29.title: Caviar: knowing your sevruga from beluga
2003-11-29.abstract: PARIS—  Gastronomically, the holiday season suggests luxury, and France's traditional lineup of end-of-year splurges include silken foie gras, earthy black truffles, bittersweet chocolates and ethereal caviar. Of the four, caviar is without doubt the priciest, the most exotic, the rarest and one of the most difficult for the average consumer to evaluate. Yet few gastronomic joys can compete with the intense, momentary pleasure of the sparkling, silken, mouth-popping eggs of the rare sturgeon from the Caspian Sea.

2003-11-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Bangladeshi's Autobiography Is Banned
2003-11-29.abstract: Budhadev Bhattacharjee, West Bengal State (India) Chief Minister, announces government ban on autobiography by Bangladeshi writer Taslima Nasreen entitled Split in Two; already banned in Bangladesh, book has been criticized as offensive to Muslims (S)

2003-11-29.title: Yesterday, Hail to the Chief; Today, Hail to the Senator
2003-11-29.abstract: Pres Bush and Sen Hillary Rodham Clinton make back-to-back visits to Iraq; American soldiers seem buoyed by visits, but Iraqis find visits irrelevant; for them, measure is whether their life would change as result; Bush stays inside heavily fortified airport complex during his two-and-a-half-hour visit; Clinton ventures outside complex, saying she came to visit troops; photo (M)

2003-11-29.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-11-29.abstract: AFGHANISTAN: U.S. Says Poppy Crop Doubled in Year; INDIA: Bangladeshis Autobiography Is Banned.

2003-11-30.title: 46 Iraqis Die in Fierce Fight Between Rebels and G.I.s
2003-11-30.abstract: Military officials called it the largest battle in the country since coalition forces toppled Saddam Husseins government.

2003-11-30.title: Scotland Takes Action To Halt Drop In Population
2003-11-30.abstract: Scotland is addressing threat posed by its low birth rate, lowest in Britain; Jack McConnell, first minister, has unveiled steps to boost population by luring back emigrants and courting immigrants, move that may be politically risky; Glasgow, whose population has dropped sharply over past 40 years, has reached deal to house asylum seekers in empty subsidized apartments around city (M)

2003-11-30.title: 7 SPANISH AGENTS AND 2 JAPANESE ARE SLAIN IN IRAQ
2003-11-30.abstract: Seven Spanish intelligence officers die as Iraqi guerrillas fire rocket-propelled grenades at their cars, Mahmudiya; two Japanese diplomats are killed in apparent ambush, Tikrit, Iraq; it is unclear whether attacks, clearly aimed at key American allies, were coordinated; Spain has troops in Iraq and Japan weighs sending noncombat forces; Lt Gen Ricardo Sanchez, speaking before attacks, says attacks on American and allies soldiers have dropped sharply in last two weeks; photos (M)

2003-11-30.title: India Announces Plans to Expand AIDS Therapy Programs
2003-11-30.abstract: The decision amounts to a significant policy shift for India, which has not previously attempted to offer antiretroviral treatment on any significant scale.

2003-11-30.title: Iraqi Governing Council Voices Support for June Elections
2003-11-30.abstract: The council agreed by a unanimous vote that full, national elections would be the best way to choose an interim government in June.
